title: Editor’s pick: Stylus Medicine - Nature Biotechnology
gist: Nature Biotechnology's annual editor's pick of a well-funded early-stage company profiles Stylus Medicine, which is engineering novel recombinases for in vivo CAR T cell cancer therapy; the article text itself is almost entirely paywalled beyond this one-sentence description.

insights.claim: Stylus Medicine is developing novel engineered recombinases as a platform for in vivo CAR T cell cancer therapy, meaning CAR T cells would be generated inside the patient's body rather than manufactured ex vivo.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: An in vivo CAR-T approach, if it works, could bypass the costly ex vivo cell manufacturing and lymphodepletion logistics that constrain current CAR-T economics and access, a structurally cheaper competing modality to watch.
insights.quote: Stylus Medicine is engineering novel recombinases for in vivo CAR T cell cancer therapy.

insights.claim: Nature Biotechnology selected Stylus Medicine as its editor's pick in a recurring annual feature that highlights companies which received sizeable early-stage funding rounds in the prior year.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Inclusion in this annual list is itself a signal that Stylus Medicine raised a notably large early-stage round in 2025, flagging it for further private-market diligence.
insights.quote: Each year, Nature Biotechnology highlights companies that received sizeable early-stage funding in the previous year.
